## Storage Tier Map

| Tier Key    | Drive | Role                         |
|-------------|-------|------------------------------|
| hot_h       | H:    | Bifrost runtime, models, logs |
| canonical_d | D:    | Git repos, project source     |
| cold_e      | E:    | Archives, PG backups, WAL     |
| backup_g    | G:    | Backup copies of E: and F:    |
| scratch_i   | I:    | Temporary scratch (auto-delete)|

## Drive State (2026-07-17)

- **D:** repos/projects/worktrees — canonical source (Git)
- **E:** cold evidence, PG18 backups, WAL archive
- **F:** hot PostgreSQL 18 data (`agent_core`, `cognee_core`)
- **G:** backup copies (E: + selective F:)
- **H:** Bifrost gateway runtime (`H:\AgentRuntime\bifrost`)
- **I:** disposable scratch (temp files only)

## Resource Registration Policy

Every durable project resource must be registered via `agentcore.register_artifact_location()`.
Temporary files on I: are exempt while temporary and must be promoted or deleted at task close.
Agents use `retrieve_context` / `build_handoff` to query location maps — never raw SQL.

## Key DB Functions

- `agentcore.v_project_resource_map` — cross-table location map view
- `agentcore.v_active_worktrees` — non-retired worktrees per project
- `agentcore.v_project_storage_tiers` — storage tier summary
- `agentcore.register_artifact_location()` — governed registration
